comments.text: I would like to share a small numerical contribution concerning the case \(k=3\), \(m=3\). I uploaded the corresponding Google Colab notebook, data files, plots, and code here: Zenodo numerical package for \(f_{3,3}(x)\). The function considered is\[ f_{3,3}(x)= \#\{1\leq n\leq x:\ n=a^3+b^3+c^3,\ a,b,c\in\mathbb Z_{\geq 0}\}. \]Using a pair-sum bitset algorithm, I computed the exact values\[ f_{3,3}(10^r),\qquad 0\leq r\leq 8. \]The obtained sequence is\[ 1,\ 6,\ 28,\ 173,\ 1352,\ 11662,\ 107875,\ 1037872,\ 10172774. \]Thus, if\[ a(r)=f_{3,3}(10^r), \]then the beginning of the proposed new sequence is\[ a(0)=1,\quad a(1)=6,\quad a(2)=28,\quad a(3)=173,\quad a(4)=1352, \]\[ a(5)=11662,\quad a(6)=107875,\quad a(7)=1037872,\quad a(8)=10172774. \]The algorithm first forms all pair sums\[ a^3+b^3\leq x, \]then marks all integers of the form\[ a^3+b^3+c^3\leq x \]in a Boolean array. This gives exact values for the above range. The observed densities\[ \frac{f_{3,3}(10^r)}{10^r} \]decrease slowly and appear numerically close to \(0.10\) for \(r=8\). A simple experimental fit suggests a possible positive-density model of the form\[ f_{3,3}(x)\approx \delta_3 x+A x^\theta, \]with numerical values approximately\[ \delta_3\approx 0.10,\qquad \theta\approx 0.68. \]This is only numerical evidence, not a proof. However, the data seem consistent with the stronger conjectural possibility that\[ f_{3,3}(x)\sim \delta_3 x \]for some positive constant \(\delta_3\), perhaps near \(0.10\). I also propose the following OEIS-type sequence associated with this problem:\[ a(r)=\#\{1\leq n\leq 10^r:\ n=x^3+y^3+z^3,\ x,y,z\in\mathbb Z_{\geq0}\}. \]The first terms are\[ 1,\ 6,\ 28,\ 173,\ 1352,\ 11662,\ 107875,\ 1037872,\ 10172774. \]The computations and plots are included in the Zenodo/Colab package linked above. Disclosure :Chatgpt 5.5 is used for assistance to predict the obtained entries (vaues up to 10^16))
